Symptoms, signs, and abnormal clinical a.Scope: Includes symptoms, signs, abnormal results, and ill-defined conditions with no diagnosis classifiable elsewhere. Signs/symptoms pointing to a specific diagnosis are classified in other chapters.

a. Use of symptom codes.rule: Use symptom/sign codes when a related definitive diagnosis has not been established by the provider.

b. Use of a symptom code with a definiti.rule: May report signs/symptoms in addition to definitive diagnosis when they are not routinely associated with that diagnosis (e.g., complex syndromes). Sequence definitive diagnosis first.
b. Use of a symptom code with a definiti.exception: Do not code signs/symptoms routinely associated with the disease process unless instructed.

c. Combination codes that include sympto.rule: When using a combination code for diagnosis and common symptoms, do not assign an additional symptom code.

d. Repeated falls.R29.6: Use when patient has recently fallen and reason is being investigated.
d. Repeated falls.Z91.81: Use when patient has history of falling and is at risk for future falls.
d. Repeated falls.note: Both codes may be used together when appropriate.

e. Coma.R40.20: Use when cause of coma is unknown or due to TBI and coma scale not documented.
e. Coma.restrictions: Do not use unspecified coma or coma scale codes for medically induced coma or sedated patients.

1) Coma Scale.usage: May be used with TBI codes; cannot be used with R40.2A.
1) Coma Scale.coding: One code from each subcategory; 7th character indicates when recorded and must match.
1) Coma Scale.reporting: At minimum, report initial score at presentation (may be from EMT or ER).
1) Coma Scale.R40.24-: Use when only total score is documented.
1) Coma Scale.timing: If multiple scores in first 24 hours, code only score at admission.

g. SIRS due to Non-Infectious Process.rule: Assign code for underlying condition first, then R65.10 (without organ dysfunction) or R65.11 (with organ dysfunction).
g. SIRS due to Non-Infectious Process.additional: Assign codes for specific organ dysfunction(s) when present.
g. SIRS due to Non-Infectious Process.query: If cause of organ dysfunction is unclear, query provider.

h. Death NOS.R99: Use only when patient is brought in already deceased and pronounced dead on arrival. Not for discharge disposition.

i. NIHSS Stroke Scale.usage: Use with acute stroke codes (I60-I63) to indicate neurological status/severity.
i. NIHSS Stroke Scale.reporting: At minimum, report initial score; multiple scores optional.
